rationale: X's core reasoning (both submit_initial/submit_update reject list prompts, so gating on already_submitted was wrong) directly mirrors yJader's actual inline comment, and its APPROVE verdict matches the two real LGTM approvals — high grounding and precision. Y covers the same technical fact but mislabels it 'major' and requests changes despite the PR being approved by all reviewers, tacks on a speculative error-propagation concern that applies equally to the pre-existing code (not something the diff introduced), and repeats its entire review verbatim three times with a messy 'Run report' dump, hurting precision and actionability.
